name: CZ CELLxGENE
description: Single-cell dataset repository and interactive explorer from the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative.

name: Gene Expression Omnibus
description: Public functional genomics database.

name: Human Cell Atlas
description: Open global atlas of all cells in the human body.

name: Single Cell PORTAL
description: Public database for single-cell RNA.

name: Single Cell Expression Atlas
description: Public database for single-cell RNA.

name: PubChem
description: One of the largest chemical databases covering compounds, genes, and proteins.

name: ChEBI
description: Database focused on small chemical compounds.

name: ChEMBL
description: Bioactive molecules with drug-like properties.

name: ChemSpider
description: Chemical structure database.

name: HMDB (Human Metabolome Database)
description: Comprehensive database of small molecule metabolites found in the human body.

name: KEGG COMPOUND
description: Collection of small molecules and biopolymers.

name: LIPID MAPS
description: Database of lipids.

name: Rhea
description: Database of chemical reactions.

name: DrugCentral
description: Online drug compendium with drug mode of action and indication information.

name: Drug Repurposing Hub
description: Collections of drug repurposing data including drug, MoA, target, etc.

name: Therapeutic Target Database
description: Drug-target, target-disease, and drug-disease datasets.

name: ZINC Ligand Discovery Database
description: Free database of commercially-available compounds for virtual screening.

name: PathwayCommons
description: Database of pathways and interactions.

name: KEGG PATHWAY
description: Collection of pathway maps.

name: WikiPathways
description: Database of biological pathways.

name: Reactome
description: Expert-curated, peer-reviewed pathway database with detailed reaction mechanisms.

name: BioCyc
description: Collection of pathway/genome databases across thousands of organisms.

name: SIGNOR
description: Database of causal signaling interactions and pathways.

name: MSigDB (Molecular Signatures Database)
description: Curated gene sets derived from pathways and biological processes.

name: MassBank
description: Open source databases and tools for mass spectrometry reference spectra.

name: MoNA MassBank of North America
description: Meta-database of metabolite mass spectra, metadata, and associated compounds.

name: THE HUMAN PROTEIN ATLAS
description: Comprehensive human protein database covering cells, tissues, and organs.

name: PROTEIN DATA BANK (PDB)
description: 3D structures of proteins, nucleic acids, and complexes.

name: UniProt
description: Functional information on proteins.

name: AlphaFold Protein Structure Database
description: 3D protein structure predictions by DeepMind.

name: CATH Database
description: Hierarchical classification of protein domain structures.

name: SAbDab
description: Structural Antibody Database containing all antibody structures in the PDB.

name: OADB (Observed Antibody Space Database)
description: Database of antibody sequences from immune repertoire sequencing.

name: ENCODE
description: Encyclopedia of DNA Elements; regulatory and functional genomic elements across the genome.

name: Ensembl
description: Genome browser and annotation database for vertebrate and other eukaryotic genomes.

name: GenBank
description: NCBI's database of genetic sequences.

name: UCSC Genome Browser
description: UCSC's genome browser for exploring genomic data.

name: cBioPortal
description: Cancer genomics database aggregating many patient datasets.

name: The Genotype-Tissue Expression (GTEx)
description: Human gene expression and regulation resource across multiple tissues.

name: Dependency Map (DepMap)
description: CRISPR-Cas9 screens in cancer cell lines.

name: Catalogue Of Somatic Mutations In Cancer (COSMIC)
description: Resource on somatic mutations in cancers.

name: JASPAR
description: Database of transcription factor binding profiles.

name: gnomAD
description: Genome Aggregation Database; genetic variation from large-scale sequencing projects.

name: Rfam
description: Database of RNA families with sequence alignments and consensus structures.

name: KEGG DRUG
description: Comprehensive, approved drug information.

name: DrugBank
description: Database of drugs and targets from the University of Alberta.

name: DisGeNET
description: Database of gene-disease associations integrating expert-curated and GWAS data.

name: OMIM (Online Mendelian Inheritance in Man)
description: Comprehensive database of human genes and genetic disorders.

name: DGIdb
description: Drug-gene interactions and the druggable genome.

name: Comparative Toxicogenomics Database
description: Chemical-gene interactions, chemical-disease and gene-disease associations, chemical-phenotype associations.

name: NCI60
description: Focuses on 60 cancer cell lines and many drugs.

name: Genomics of Drug Sensitivity in Cancer (GDSC)
description: Drug sensitivity for approximately 1000 human cancer cell lines and hundreds of compounds.

name: Cancer Cell Line Encyclopedia
description: Database of approximately 1000 cancer cell lines.

name: CellMiner Cross Database (CellMinerCDB)
description: Integrates multiple cancer cell line databases.

name: STITCH
description: Chemical-protein interactions.

name: BindingDB
description: Compounds and target database for binding affinity data.

name: PDBBind
description: Binding affinity data for biomolecular complexes.

name: STRING
description: PPI networks for multiple organisms.

name: BioGRID
description: Protein, genetic, and chemical interactions.

name: IntAct
description: Open-source molecular interaction database and analysis system from EMBL-EBI.

name: Drug Mechanism Database (DrugMechDB)
description: Mechanisms of action from drug to disease.

name: DRKG
description: Large-scale biological knowledge graph for drug discovery.

name: Hetionet
description: Heterogeneous network integrating genes, diseases, drugs, pathways, and more.

name: PrimeKG
description: Multi-modal precision medicine knowledge graph integrating clinical, genetic, and drug data.

name: ClinicalTrials.gov
description: Privately and publicly funded clinical studies.

name: MIMIC-IV
description: Freely accessible critical care database.

name: CrossDocked2020
description: Large-scale dataset for structure-based virtual screening.

name: GuacaMol
description: Benchmark suite for generative molecular design models.

name: MoleculeNet
description: Benchmark datasets for molecular machine learning.

name: MOSES
description: Benchmarking platform for molecular generation models.

name: OpenBioLink
description: Benchmark datasets for biological knowledge graph completion.

name: Therapeutics Data Commons
description: Datasets for drug-target, response, drug-drug interaction, etc.

name: PubMed E-utilities
description: APIs for searching and retrieving biomedical literature from PubMed.

name: NCBI E-utilities
description: Unified APIs for accessing NCBI databases (Gene, GEO, SRA, PubChem, etc).

name: UniProt REST API
description: Programmatic access to protein sequence and functional annotation data.

name: Ensembl REST API
description: API for genomic annotations, variants, genes, and comparative genomics.

name: KEGG REST API
description: API for accessing KEGG pathways, compounds, genes, and reactions.

name: ChEMBL Web Services
description: REST API for bioactive molecules, targets, and bioassays.

name: Open Targets Platform API
description: API for target–disease associations integrating genetics, genomics, and drug data.

name: ClinicalTrials.gov API
description: API for querying clinical trial metadata and results.

name: Chemistry Development Kit
description: Cheminformatics software and machine learning tools.

name: Biopython
description: Collection of Python tools for biological computation including sequence analysis, structure parsing, and database access.

name: RDKit
description: Cheminformatics software and machine learning toolkit.

name: DeepChem
description: Deep learning library for drug discovery, quantum chemistry, and materials science.

name: Scanpy
description: Python library for scRNA-seq analysis.

name: Seurat
description: R library for scRNA-seq analysis.

name: scvi-tools
description: Probabilistic models for single-cell omics data analysis.

name: CellTypist
description: Automated cell type annotation for scRNA-seq.

name: Squidpy
description: Python library for spatial single-cell analysis.

name: GROMACS
description: Molecular dynamics simulation package for biochemical molecules.

name: MDAnalysis
description: Python library for analyzing and altering molecular dynamics simulation trajectories.

name: OpenMM
description: High-performance toolkit for molecular simulation and GPU-accelerated MD.

name: FlashDeconv
description: High-performance spatial transcriptomics deconvolution (~1M spots in ~3 min).

name: ChatSpatial
description: MCP server for spatial transcriptomics analysis via natural language.

name: drGAT
description: Attention-based model for drug response prediction with gene explainability.

name: MOFGCN
description: GCN combined with heterogeneous network for drug response prediction.

name: DGDRP
description: Multi-view embedding neural network for drug response prediction.

name: DeepAEG
description: GNN embedding with attention mechanism for drug response prediction.

name: DeepPurpose
description: Deep learning library for drug repurposing.

name: NeoDTI
description: Library for drug-target interaction prediction using network topology.

name: DTINet
description: Network-based framework integrating heterogeneous biological data for DTI prediction.

name: DeepDTA
description: Deep learning model using CNNs on protein sequences and drug SMILES for drug-target affinity.

name: GraphDTA
description: Graph neural network–based DTI prediction using molecular graphs.

name: MolTrans
description: Transformer-based DTI model leveraging molecular substructures.

name: DrugBAN
description: Bilinear attention network for interpretable DTI prediction.

name: MCPINN
description: Drug discovery via compound-protein interaction and machine learning.

name: TransformerCPI
description: CPI prediction using Transformer architecture.

name: REINVENT
description: Reinforcement learning for de novo drug design.

name: MolGPT
description: Transformer-based model for molecular generation.

name: Molecular Transformer
description: Sequence-to-sequence model for retrosynthesis prediction.

name: TargetDiff
description: 3D equivariant diffusion model for structure-based drug design.

name: ChemLLM-7B-Chat
description: Large language model for chemical and molecular science.

name: BioGPT
description: Large language model for biomedical text generation.

name: GeneGPT
description: LLM for biomedical information, integrated with various APIs.

name: GenePT
description: Foundation LLM for single-cell data.

name: scPRINT
description: Pretrained on 50M cells for scRNA-seq denoising and zero imputation.

name: ClawBio
description: Bioinformatics-native AI agent skill library with local-first pharmacogenomics, ancestry PCA, semantic similarity, nutrigenomics, and metagenomics skills.

name: scFoundation
description: Large-scale foundation model for single-cell gene expression, enabling multiple downstream tasks.

name: scGPT
description: Transformer-based foundation model pretrained on millions of single-cell profiles.

name: Geneformer
description: Context-aware, attention-based deep learning model pretrained on a large corpus of single-cell transcriptomes.

name: BulkFormer
description: Foundation model for bulk RNA-seq data; learns general transcriptomic representations.

name: scBERT
description: BERT-based foundation model pretrained on large-scale scRNA-seq data for cell type annotation.

name: CellPLM
description: Cell pre-trained language model with inter-cell transformer architecture for diverse single-cell analysis tasks.

name: GigaPath
description: Slide-level digital pathology foundation model pretrained on 1.3 billion pathology image tokens from whole-slide images.

name: UNI
description: General-purpose self-supervised pathology foundation model trained on 100K+ whole-slide images for diverse computational pathology tasks.

name: CONCH
description: Vision-language foundation model for computational pathology trained with contrastive captioning on pathology image–text pairs.

name: Phikon
description: ViT-based pathology foundation model pretrained with iBOT self-supervision on TCGA whole-slide images.

name: scMulan
description: Single-cell multi-omic language model pretrained on approximately 10M cells spanning transcriptomics, epigenomics, and proteomics.

name: MIRA
description: Probabilistic multimodal topic model jointly modeling single-cell transcriptomics and chromatin accessibility for regulatory network inference.

name: GLUE
description: Graph-Linked Unified Embedding framework for unpaired single-cell multi-omics data integration across RNA, ATAC, methylation, and protein modalities.

name: BABEL
description: Cross-modality translation model enabling prediction between scRNA-seq and scATAC-seq profiles.

name: MOFA+
description: Multi-Omics Factor Analysis framework identifying shared axes of variation across bulk and single-cell datasets.

name: GeneCompass
description: Large-scale foundation model integrating DNA regulatory sequences and single-cell transcriptomics from 120M+ cells across multiple species.

name: SpatialGlue
description: Graph attention network for spatial multi-omics integration jointly embedding spatial transcriptomics with chromatin accessibility or proteomics.

name: MIDAS
description: Mosaic integration and differential accessibility model for single-cell multi-omics data with missing-modality support.

name: scArches
description: Transfer learning framework for mapping new single-cell datasets onto pre-trained reference atlases across batches, conditions, and modalities.

name: TOSICA
description: Transformer-based framework for one-stop interpretable cell-type annotation supporting cross-dataset and cross-species transfer.

name: Evolutionary Scale Modeling (ESM)
description: Protein language model for generating embeddings and structure prediction.

name: ChemBERTa-2
description: Chemical embeddings and prediction using BERT-based architecture.

name: AlphaFold3
description: Predicts structures of proteins, nucleic acids, small molecules, and their complexes.

name: Boltz-1
description: Open-source all-atom biomolecular structure prediction model achieving AlphaFold3-level accuracy.

name: Chai-1
description: Unified molecular structure prediction model covering proteins, nucleic acids, small molecules, and complexes.

name: ESM3
description: Multimodal protein language model that jointly reasons over sequence, structure, and function for generative protein design.

name: RFdiffusion
description: Generative model for protein backbone design using diffusion.

name: ProteinMPNN
description: Deep learning model for protein sequence design given backbone structure.

name: OmegaFold
description: High-resolution de novo protein structure prediction from sequence.

name: RoseTTAFold
description: Three-track neural network for protein structure prediction.

name: CHIEF
description: Clinical Histopathology Imaging Evaluation Foundation model integrating histology images and clinical context for pan-cancer analysis.

name: BiomedCLIP
description: CLIP-based vision-language foundation model for biomedical images and text trained on PubMed figure–caption pairs.

name: Nucleotide Transformer
description: Foundation model for genomic sequences across multiple species.

name: DNABERT
description: Pre-trained bidirectional encoder for DNA sequence analysis.

name: DNABERT-2
description: Improved genome foundation model with efficient tokenization.

name: Enformer
description: Transformer model predicting gene expression from DNA sequence.

name: Basenji
description: Sequential regulatory activity prediction from DNA sequences.

name: Caduceus
description: Bidirectional equivariant long-range DNA sequence model based on Mamba.

name: Evo
description: Long-context genomic foundation model supporting up to 1M tokens.

name: HyenaDNA
description: Long-range genomic foundation model handling sequences up to 1M tokens with sub-quadratic attention.
